findings.summary: Component counts and types are accurate for SparkFun Teensy Ethernet Adapter; TX+/TX-/RX+/RX- Ethernet magnetics signals misclassified as UART; Ethernet interface not detected despite B10601S 10BAS...

correct.description: The schematic correctly reports 21 total components: 3 capacitors, 4 resistors (75 ohm), 2 connectors (J1 SWD, J2 RJ45), 1 IC (U1 B10601S magnetics), 4 standoffs, 4 fiducials, 3 logos/graphics, 1 no-connect. BOM groupings match source schematic. Power rail GND-only is correct for an adapter board powered externally by the Teensy.

The signal_analysis correctly identifies four differential pairs: RD+/RD-, TD+/TD-, TX+/TX-, RX+/RX-. These correspond to the 10BASE-T magnetics module interface. The pairs share J1 and J2 with U1 as expected. The esd_protection attribution to U1 is reasonable since the magnetics provide galvanic isolation.

Four RC low-pass filters are detected (R5, R6, R7, R8 each 75 ohm with C1 1nF, fc=2.12 MHz). Topologically correct: R7/R8 connect TXCT/RXCT pins to a node with C1, and R5/R6 connect unused RJ45 pairs (4,5 and 7,8) to the same center-tap node. While these are better described as Ethernet center-tap terminators than generic RC filters, the topology detection is valid.

The subcircuits list correctly groups U1 (B10601S) with neighbors C2 (0.1uF TCT decoupling), C4 (0.1uF RCT decoupling), J1 (SWD/device-side connector), J2 (RJ45), R7 (75 ohm RXCT terminator), and R8 (75 ohm TXCT terminator). R5 and R6 (unused pair termination resistors) are not in the subcircuit which is acceptable.

incorrect.description: The bus_analysis.uart section detects TX+, TX-, RX+, RX- as UART signals. These are Ethernet magnetics signals from the B10601S 10BASE-T transformer module (TX and RX differential pairs at the line side). The pin names on U1 are explicitly 'TX+', 'TX-', 'RX+', 'RX-' and connect to an RJ45 connector (J2), not to any UART-capable device. This is a false positive triggered by the TX/RX net name pattern matching.

missed.description: ethernet_interfaces is empty ([]) even though U1 (B10601S) is a 10BASE-T Ethernet magnetics module with TX+, TX-, RX+, RX- (line-side differential pairs) and TD+, TD-, RD+, RD- (device-side connections to Teensy SWD header), plus TXCT/RXCT center-tap pins. The RJ45 connector J2 with TX+/TX-/RX+/RX- should trigger Ethernet interface detection.

findings.summary: PCB footprint count and layer configuration are accurate; PCB net count matches schematic net count (18 nets); Zone analysis correctly identifies GND copper fill and keepout zone

correct.description: 36 footprints correctly enumerated: 21 schematic in_bom components + 1 on-board non-BOM item (G3 OSHW logo) + 14 kibuzzard decorative silkscreen items. 2-layer board (F.Cu, B.Cu) with 88 track segments, 10 vias, 2 zones (1 keepout, 1 GND fill on B.Cu). Board dimensions 39.37 x 29.21 mm. Routing complete with 0 unrouted nets.

The PCB reports 18 nets which precisely matches the schematic's 18 total_nets. Named nets include GND, RD+, RD-, TD+, TD-, TX+, TX-, RX+, RX-, LED, plus unnamed internal nets. Net count consistency between schematic and PCB is confirmed.

Two zones correctly identified: one keepout zone spanning both copper layers (net=0, unfilled, with no-via and no-pad restrictions), and one GND fill on B.Cu (filled, 1148 mm2 area). This matches what is visible in the source kicad_pcb file.
